task: Run project-onboarder in the correct propickle-buddy repo
validation: inspected AGENTS.md, .ai router/context/registry, README, server/web package files, extension manifest, planning docs
errors: initial onboarding pass was run in the placeholder OneDrive folder before repo path was corrected
lessons: Use C:\Users\nguye\Downloads\propickle-buddy as the project root for this repo
next: Confirm whether to commit the new .ai workflow files, then continue with the next feature branch for a second venue.

task: Migrate availability cache, APIs, share page, and webhook helpers into one Next.js app deployment
validation: production smoke on localhost:3018: health 200, POST sync 200, share text 200 with interval, share HTML 200 with interval, wrong share page 404, wrong public API 404
errors: Initial direction used a two-service web/backend split, then user clarified they wanted literally one deployment website.
Next page notFound rendered HTTP 200 for invalid share tokens, so web/proxy.ts now rejects invalid /s links before rendering.
lessons: Use web/ as the full-stack Next.js deployment: extension reads, Next API stores, Next share page renders, future bots reuse the same cache/formatter modules.
next: Deploy web/ to Vercel with Supabase env vars, then set extension Backend URL and Share URL base to the Vercel app URL.

task: Fix Vercel build failure from import-time Supabase storage guard
validation: VERCEL=1 with empty Supabase env npm.cmd --prefix web run build
errors: Vercel imports Next route modules during build/page-data collection, so throwing at module evaluation failed deploy before runtime env handling.
lessons: Deployed-storage requirements should be enforced at runtime storage calls, not top-level module import, so Next builds can compile routes safely.
next: Redeploy latest main commit on Vercel; still set Supabase env vars for runtime cache behavior.

task: Add Stitch-designed public homepage with light/dark mode and Three.js court hero
validation: Stitch generated desktop screen 9173065ce74848ec8905126b221fde43 and mobile screen 6bd011d7aeb746f98857f10bf8ec2795
saved raw Stitch HTML and screenshots under imported/homepage
browser desktop visual QA for / with Three.js canvas present
browser mobile visual QA at 390x844 with 48px CTA tap targets
errors: Browser runtime blocked direct navigation to /health, so route smoke used local HTTP requests instead.
Canvas getContext was unavailable in the browser read-only evaluation wrapper, so canvas rendering was verified by dimensions and screenshot.
lessons: For Stitch imports, keep raw artifacts in imported/homepage and implement the production surface with local CSS/components instead of Tailwind CDN exports.
next: Review the homepage in browser, then commit and push the feature when the visual direction is accepted.

task: Add Broadway Pickleball ClubSpark venue support
validation: POST sample Broadway payload to /api/availability/broadway returned ok
browser smoke /s/dev-share/broadway showed Broadway theme/note/direct ClubSpark link
lessons: ClubSpark Broadway exposes public guest slots via book-interval.not-booked and edit-booking elements with data-test-id values containing resource id, ISO date, and minutes.
next: Reload the unpacked extension so the new host permission/provider is active, then run a real Broadway refresh from the extension.

task: Wire share-page venue switching on Broadway branch after absorbing main day-level booking button change
validation: fast-forwarded feature/broadway-venue to main commit ac8e6c0
local smoke confirmed /s/dev-share/propickle contains /s/dev-share/broadway and /s/dev-share/broadway contains /s/dev-share/propickle
confirmed ports 3007 and 3011 closed after smoke
errors: Initial local smoke command over-escaped href matching, then a temporary 3011 dev server collided with an already-running Next dev server on 3007.
lessons: With two venues, a single opposite-venue link is enough; defer dropdown/theme-speed preferences to a later UX branch.
next: Manual extension QA: reload unpacked extension, read Broadway as guest, confirm 9-day synced share page and venue switch in the browser.

task: Diagnose share-page venue switch and remove unused nav icon
validation: local HTML smoke confirmed Broadway renders data-venue-theme=broadway with Broadway accent color
local HTML smoke confirmed ProPickle and Broadway share pages link to each other
local HTML smoke confirmed stitch-refresh icon removed
lessons: The deployed/main share page can still show ProPickle fallback styling until the Broadway branch is committed, merged, and deployed.
next: Commit/push Broadway branch, deploy, then verify production share page is no longer serving old main code.

task: Replace two-venue shortcut with scalable share-page venue selector
validation: HTML smoke confirmed Broadway page has venue menu, venue section, ProPickle link, current Broadway marker, and mobile Booking action
in-app browser preview confirmed #venues lists ProPickle and Broadway with Broadway marked current
errors: Browser navigation waited indefinitely on full load until using a short navigation race; the page itself rendered correctly.
lessons: The share page venue control must render all registered venues, not an opposite-venue shortcut, so adding a third venue is data-only.
next: Inspect the preview, then commit and push the Broadway branch when accepted.

task: Clean share-page venue and metric UX
validation: HTML smoke confirmed Open Days, Open Hours, and Next Open metrics render
HTML smoke confirmed duplicate in-page venues section removed
HTML smoke confirmed desktop and mobile venue menus still render
lessons: Availability summary should count bookable days, not raw interval count; venue switching belongs in nav, not duplicated as page content.
next: Preview visually, then commit/push Broadway branch when accepted.

task: Replace fake share-page brand mark with venue logo assets and remove Schedule nav
validation: HTML smoke confirmed Schedule/Schedules nav text removed and Venues remains
errors: Direct ProPickle HTML fetch is blocked by Cloudflare, so the ProPickle mark uses the clean user-provided PNG asset.
lessons: Use actual venue logo assets for brand marks; remove nav items that only jump to the current section.
next: Visually inspect the refreshed preview, then commit/push the Broadway branch.

task: Run Broadway branch merge-readiness QA
validation: production smoke on localhost:3012: health ok, homepage 200, Broadway share 200/theme/logo/note/metrics/tooltips/venue menu/booking date link, Broadway public API ready, Broadway text endpoint 200, ProPickle empty share themed with logo/menu, wrong share token 404
errors: PowerShell reserved HOME variable caused first smoke script attempt to abort before route assertions; rerun passed with a renamed variable.
Direct public ClubSpark HTML fetch confirms page shell/theme/logo but not slot markers because availability is client-rendered.
lessons: Automated QA can prove share rendering and backend routes; final live availability confidence still needs one manual extension read after reloading manifest permissions.
next: Reload unpacked extension, run one real Broadway read as guest, then commit/push/merge if the share page receives 9 days.

task: Fix ClubSpark day-load race and run Ponytail audit
lessons: For ClubSpark hash navigation, do not treat the URL hash alone as proof that the schedule DOM has rerendered.
next: Manual Broadway extension read should confirm multi-day loads still progress without stale-day data.

task: Apply Ponytail simplification cuts after Broadway review
lessons: A single web venue registry can feed share links, themes, and server fallback metadata without adding a framework layer.
next: Manual Broadway extension read, then commit and push if the real page still syncs correctly.

task: Add North Ryde Mindbody venue support
validation: Headless Chrome smoke: Mindbody Standard and Premium 30-minute First Available read returned 2 days with merged open intervals
errors: npm.cmd --prefix web run typecheck failed without --incremental false because Windows denied writing web/tsconfig.tsbuildinfo.
npm.cmd --prefix web run build is blocked locally because Windows denies opening generated web/.next/trace and web/.next/trace-build.
lessons: North Ryde uses Mindbody appointments; staffId=any_staff gives an aggregate First Available schedule, so the provider can avoid iterating every court.
next: Reload the unpacked extension permissions, run a real North Ryde 9-day read, then clear the local .next lock before production build if needed.

task: Clarify any-court versus same-court availability intervals
lessons: Merged open_intervals are any-court windows; only providers with court/resource identity should emit same_court_intervals.
next: Reload the extension and re-read Broadway to populate same-court runs; ProPickle and North Ryde will show the continuity fallback until their providers can prove court identity.

task: Run merge-readiness QA for North Ryde and interval semantics branch
validation: production smoke on localhost temp cache: health/home/share pages ok, seeded northryde POST ok, northryde ready share ok, public API ready/theme ok, wrong token 404
errors: Initial public API smoke for uncached North Ryde returned 404 by design; rerun seeded an isolated local cache before checking the ready path.
lessons: Share pages can render friendly empty states while public JSON endpoints correctly return 404 until cache exists.
next: Fast-forward merge feature/north-ryde-venue into main, then push main when ready for Vercel deployment.

task: Restore visible North Ryde service levels on share pages
validation: production smoke with temp cache: North Ryde share renders Booking levels, Standard/Premium names, prices, public API levelIntervals, and text endpoint level summary
lessons: North Ryde availability has service-level meaning, so the UI should show Standard/Premium groupings instead of only the flattened any-court interval.
next: Push the follow-up fix; reload/re-read North Ryde after Vercel deploy so cached payloads include level_intervals.

task: Add provider/resource continuity tabs for venue day cards
validation: production smoke with temp cache: North Ryde share renders Overview/Levels/Courts tab shell, public API exposes levels and courts/providers, text endpoint includes levels and courts/providers
lessons: For Mindbody, First Available is only the overview; real continuity requires provider/resource-specific reads grouped by service level.
next: After deployment, reload the extension and re-read North Ryde to populate same_court_intervals from provider/resource reads.

task: QA provider/resource continuity tabs preview
validation: in-app browser QA clicked Levels, Courts, and Continuity tabs successfully
errors: Browser DOM snapshot helper failed in this environment; targeted browser reads/clicks were used instead.
lessons: Do not cache raw provider debug slots/errors unless the UI or diagnostics consume them.
next: Keep preview running for manual inspection, then reload/re-read North Ryde after Vercel deploy.

task: Clarify provider continuity read failures and segmented controls
validation: seeded production smoke: public API exposes continuityStatus available/failed and text endpoint reports provider read failure
errors: Raw HTML smoke could not see the client-only Continuity panel until interaction; narrowed automated smoke to API/text path.
lessons: Provider/resource continuity needs a small status field so failed reads are not misrepresented as unsupported booking-page data.
next: Push the fix, wait for Vercel, then run a real North Ryde refresh when ready despite its longer 16+5 resource runtime.

task: Make North Ryde refresh fast by default with optional deep court scan
lessons: North Ryde same-court continuity is expensive because the Mindbody widget is stateful; keep first-available refresh fast and make provider/court scanning explicit.
next: Reload the unpacked extension and test North Ryde Refresh Venue first; use Deep Scan Courts only when same-court continuity is needed.

task: Warn before deep court scans and keep saved share actions available during refresh
lessons: Long-running refreshes should not hide already-synced share actions; users can keep using the last good cache while a new scan runs.
next: Next candidate from the backlog: a proper background refresh job/status model for refresh-all or selected-venue refreshes.

task: Add background refresh jobs for selected/all venue refreshes
lessons: Refresh selected/all can use one sequential background job with popup polling; remove the legacy direct refresh message so there is one refresh path.
next: Reload the unpacked extension, test Refresh Selected and Refresh All, and keep Deep Scan Courts for explicit slow continuity reads.

task: Move popup venue picker into refresh target controls
lessons: The venue selector should read as a refresh target, not as a global filter in the popup header.
next: Reload the unpacked extension and verify Refresh Selected uses the target picker.

task: Expose refresh job failure details and retry North Ryde in focused tab
lessons: Refresh-all summaries need per-venue failure detail; North Ryde Mindbody may need a focused-tab retry because the widget can under-render in inactive tabs.
next: Reload the unpacked extension, run Refresh All again, and use the displayed North Ryde error if it still fails.

task: Make refresh all cache-first for slow North Ryde reads
lessons: Refresh All should reuse recent cache for slow stateful widgets; keep Refresh Selected as the explicit force-fresh path.
next: Reload the unpacked extension, run Refresh All twice, and confirm the second run says recent cache reused for North Ryde.

task: Handle Mindbody fully-booked schedule state for North Ryde
lessons: Mindbody fully-booked days use different date copy from availability days; treat them as valid schedule pages with zero slots so scans can advance.
next: Reload the unpacked extension and rerun Refresh Selected for North Ryde from the fully-booked state.

task: Polish extension refresh UX without adding dashboard bloat
lessons: Refresh UX should show per-venue freshness and keep last-good actions available, while slow/deep-scan explanations stay in tooltip-level copy.
next: Reload the unpacked extension and verify the popup shows saved results, compact controls, loader during refresh, and usable share actions while busy.

task: Tighten refresh cache timing and parallelize refresh all
lessons: Refresh All can run venue reads concurrently now that jobs are backgrounded, but Deep Scan should stay single-venue to avoid many provider/court reads at once.
next: Reload the unpacked extension, run Refresh All, and verify multiple venue tabs progress together while North Ryde cache expires after 5 minutes.

task: Show refresh timings and simplify loader animation
lessons: Use final refresh timings to make multi-venue scraping feel observable; keep loader motion to text dots instead of animating the paddle/ball art.
next: Reload the unpacked extension, run Refresh All, and confirm final status shows per-venue timings plus a cleaner racket-ball Loading indicator.

task: Add refresh stale as the smart multi-venue default
lessons: Keep force-refresh controls available but make stale-only refresh the default multi-venue action to reduce unnecessary scraping.
next: Reload the unpacked extension and verify Refresh Stale only runs venues with missing or older-than-5-minute saved results.

task: Adapt provided pickleball bounce loader for MV3 popup
lessons: Design snippets can be adapted to the no-build MV3 popup by translating React structure into plain markup and CSS-only animation.
next: Reload the unpacked extension and visually check the compact paddle/arc/ball loader during Refresh Stale or Refresh All.

task: Align share page stale freshness with extension cache window
lessons: Share page freshness warnings should use the same stale window as extension refresh controls so users see consistent cache age semantics.
next: Discuss scheduled/background refresh separately before changing scraping frequency.

task: QA refresh UX, add refresh history, polish popup controls, and update docs/context
validation: baseline extension syntax and manifest checks
baseline npm.cmd --prefix web run typecheck -- --incremental false
all extension syntax checks and manifest validation
lessons: Keep refresh history as a capped extension-storage job summary and keep lower-frequency actions in a More actions drawer to avoid popup button bloat.
next: Reload the unpacked extension and manually verify Refresh Stale, Refresh Selected, More actions, and Recent refreshes before choosing the next venue.

local homepage smoke found 4 venues and research queue
local Sydney share empty-state smoke passed

lessons: Playtomic is best handled as a same-origin public JSON provider from the Playtomic club page; PodPlay and Hamlet need separate provider spikes because they involve anonymous auth/bootstrap flows.
next: Reload the unpacked extension for new Playtomic host permissions, then run Refresh Selected for Sydney Racquet Club. Next venue candidate: House of Pickle DOM provider, then WOTSO Hamlet provider.

next: Reload the unpacked extension for the new House host permission, open House of Pickle Darling Harbour, and run Refresh Selected to verify real rendered rows.

task: Make saved results scrollable, add share-page refresh controls, abbreviate House of Pickle, and add WOTSO Pyrmont

task: Stop extension popup layout jitter after saved-results scroll change
validation: rg -n "calc\([^)]*\*|100vw|@media \(min-width|width: min\(" extension/popup.css returned no matches
lessons: Chrome extension popups should avoid viewport-derived body widths and unsupported calc multiplication; fixed popup width plus internal scroll regions is more stable.
next: Reload the unpacked extension and confirm the popup no longer resizes while saved results load or refresh polling runs.

task: Make web venue pickers current, scrollable, and default homepage to dark
errors: Initial build hit EPERM writing web/.next/trace under local Windows permissions; reran the same build with elevated filesystem access and it passed.
lessons: Homepage venue cards should derive from the central web venue registry so new venues appear on the landing page and share-page picker together.
next: Open / and /s/<shareToken>/<venueId>; confirm the homepage starts dark and both venue lists scroll cleanly with all six venues.

task: QA current diff with GSD code-review and Ponytail cleanup

errors: Initial build hit EPERM writing web/.next/trace under local Windows permissions; reran the same build with elevated filesystem access and it passed.
lessons: Keep homepage venue summaries in the web venue registry, and keep WOTSO booking windows anchored to the venue timezone rather than the browser timezone.
next: Manual QA the unpacked extension popup and WOTSO refresh in Chrome before committing/pushing.

task: Move share actions under refresh controls and collapse refresh history by default
lessons: Keep secondary popup actions close to the refresh controls and collapse history/details by default to reduce vertical noise.
next: Reload the unpacked extension and visually confirm the action row and Recent refreshes drawer.

task: Retune WOTSO share-page theme from warm orange to WOTSO green, white, and black branding
validation: all extension syntax checks and manifest validation
errors: Initial build hit EPERM writing web/.next/trace-build under local Windows permissions; reran the same build with elevated filesystem access and it passed.
lessons: Use WOTSO's green/white/black visual cues rather than generic warm sports colors.
next: Reload the unpacked extension for WOTSO permissions and verify /s/<shareToken>/wotso-pyrmont visually after sync.

task: Polish refresh UX status/timing and add availability formatter fixture tests
errors: Initial build hit EPERM writing web/.next/trace under local Windows permissions; reran the same build with elevated filesystem access and it passed.
lessons: Keep refresh timing visible in the popup history/status and protect the any-court versus same-court text summary with zero-dependency fixture tests.
next: Reload the unpacked extension, run Refresh Selected and Refresh All once, and confirm recent refresh rows show timing details clearly.

task: Fix refresh-all confirmation visibility, share venue menu dismissal, themed scrollbars, and capture planner idea
errors: Initial build hit EPERM writing web/.next/trace-build under local Windows permissions; reran the same build with elevated filesystem access and it passed.
lessons: Do not rely on native confirm dialogs inside the extension popup; use visible in-popup confirmation state for risky refresh actions.
next: Reload the unpacked extension and click Refresh all once to see Confirm Refresh all, then click outside the share-page venue menu to verify it closes.

task: Show last per-venue refresh outcome and timing in popup saved-results list
lessons: Venue rows are easier to trust when they show both saved cache age and the latest refresh result, especially failed/setup states.
next: Reload the unpacked extension and run a mixed refresh so rows show ok, cached, setup, or failed timing states.

task: Make share-page refresh trigger extension availability updates instead of page reloads
errors: Initial build hit EPERM writing web/.next/trace under local Windows permissions; reran the same build with elevated filesystem access and it passed.
lessons: Share-page refresh should route through the extension refresh job and refresh the server-rendered page only after web sync succeeds.
next: Reload the unpacked extension, open a share page, click Refresh availability, and confirm the page updates after the venue read completes.

task: Review and harden share-page refresh completion handling
errors: Initial build hit EPERM writing web/.next/trace under local Windows permissions; reran the same build with elevated filesystem access and it passed.
lessons: Share-page live reload should only happen when the active refresh job includes the requested venue and that venue syncs successfully.
next: Manual QA: start one venue refresh, click another venue's share-page Refresh, and confirm it reports another refresh is running instead of reloading stale data.

task: QA and fix House of Pickle availability hours
validation: live read-only Playwright QA against House of Pickle page for 9 days
errors: Initial build hit EPERM writing web/.next/trace under local Windows permissions; reran the same build with elevated filesystem access and it passed.
lessons: HoP exposes 30-minute rows but has a one-hour minimum booking duration; filter displayed merged windows by min duration and wait for date-button page text to settle before reading each day.
next: Reload the unpacked extension, refresh House of Pickle DH, and confirm the share page shows 9 days with no standalone 30-minute islands.

task: Clarify same-court continuity wording on share pages and text summaries
errors: Initial build hit EPERM writing web/.next/trace under local Windows permissions; reran the same build with elevated filesystem access and it passed.
lessons: User-facing continuity labels should talk about exact court continuity, not vague provider/resource terminology.
next: Reload the share page after deploy and confirm day cards say Continuity and Same-court detail instead of Courts / providers.

task: Make Refresh selected support multi-venue checkbox targets in the popup
lessons: Use the existing saved-results list as the refresh picker instead of adding another bulky venue selector.
next: Reload the unpacked extension and verify ticking multiple saved-result rows changes the button to Refresh N selected and starts one multi-venue job.

task: Run multi-venue refresh jobs concurrently with a small cap
lessons: Parallel refresh progress needs serialized storage writes so popup progress cannot regress when workers finish close together.
next: Manual QA a 3+ venue Refresh selected run and confirm at most three booking tabs are active at once while deep scan remains single-venue.

task: Mark continuity support explicitly for ProPickle, Broadway, and Sydney providers
lessons: Providers that already know whether they have exact court labels should emit continuity_status so the share page does not infer vague fallback copy.
next: Refresh Broadway and Sydney after reloading the extension and verify Continuity tab copy matches exact court availability.

task: Document when2meet-style pickleball planner options and recommended v1
lessons: Planner v1 should use secret event links before accounts to preserve the current low-auth product shape.
next: When ready, turn docs/when2meet-planner.md into a scoped UI/data-model phase.

task: Show safe venue cache freshness on the public homepage
errors: Initial build hit EPERM writing web/.next/trace-build under local Windows permissions; reran the same build with elevated filesystem access and it passed.
lessons: Public homepage cache status must stay token-free and dynamic so freshness is not frozen at build time.
next: After deploy, open the homepage and verify venue cards show Fresh cache, Stale cache, No cache, or Cache status off without exposing share links.

task: Make multi-venue refresh parallelism explicit in job state and popup history
lessons: Store the refresh job parallel limit so users can verify a multi-venue refresh is using parallel workers instead of reading as sequential progress.
next: Reload the unpacked extension, tick at least two venues, and confirm the running status says Up to N venues run at once.

task: Move popup loader/status into refresh panel and allow venue names to wrap
lessons: Refresh feedback belongs with the selectable venue refresh controls, and venue names should wrap rather than clip in the popup.
next: Reload the unpacked extension and confirm long venue names wrap cleanly while loader/status/history appear under refresh buttons.

task: Fix share-page header alignment for long venue names and clarify popup refresh confirmation
validation: all venue header visual metrics at 42 viewport/venue combinations
share venue menu outside-click and Escape QA
errors: Next dev server on 3007 exited because another dev server was already running on 3018; used 3018 for visual QA.
lessons: Long share-page venue names need laptop-width header QA, not only mobile checks.
next: Commit/push after review, then continue non-planning cleanup or move to the next concrete venue issue.

task: Fix text summary venue lookup for current venue ids
lessons: Text/bot helpers must be updated when venue ids change, not only share-page routes and themes.
next: When chat bot work resumes, add a small route-level smoke test for /s/:token/:venue/text or Messenger formatting.

task: Add repo-local extension QA playbook
errors: Creating .ai/playbooks required approved filesystem access because the sandbox blocked directory creation.
lessons: Manual Chrome extension QA belongs in a repo playbook before becoming a hook or global skill.
next: Use .ai/playbooks/extension-qa.md before pushing extension refresh or popup changes.

task: Add conservative ProPickle continuity extraction from Playbypoint detail options
validation: synthetic Playbypoint DOM smoke: detail court labels create same-court intervals
synthetic Playbypoint DOM smoke: missing detail labels keeps not_exposed
lessons: Playbypoint continuity can be read only when the Select Detail step exposes court labels after selecting an open time; keep not_exposed fallback when labels are hidden.
next: Reload the unpacked extension while logged into ProPickle, refresh ProPickle, and verify the Continuity tab shows same-court runs if Playbypoint exposes court labels.

task: Fix ProPickle continuity over-counting unavailable courts
lessons: Playbypoint can keep unavailable court detail options visible; same-court extraction must filter disabled, inactive, unavailable, full, low-opacity, and pointer-events-none options instead of trusting visibility alone.
next: Reload the unpacked extension, refresh ProPickle for the reported day, and confirm a 9-11pm interval appears only under the actually available court.

task: Disable unsafe ProPickle same-court inference
lessons: For ProPickle, the Select Detail court list can represent possible courts rather than per-time availability, so exposing same-court continuity from that DOM is false precision.
next: Find a reliable Playbypoint request/API signal before re-enabling ProPickle court continuity.

task: Fix ProPickle same-court continuity by probing accepted court detail selections per time
lessons: For Playbypoint BookBox, correct per-court continuity requires selecting each time then probing each court/detail option; labels alone are possible courts, not proof of availability.
next: Reload the extension and verify the Thursday 16 split in Chrome: Court 4 9-10pm, other courts 10-11pm.

task: Harden ProPickle continuity regression coverage for visually selected invalid courts
lessons: A Playbypoint court option may visually select while the next step remains blocked; continuity tests should require the provider to ignore that false positive.
next: Use the real ProPickle page to confirm the Thursday 16 split before marking the goal complete.

task: Add ProPickle probe diagnostics for real-page continuity mismatches
lessons: When a DOM provider cannot be verified directly by Codex, save non-public probe diagnostics in the payload so real Chrome runs can explain accepted vs rejected court/time pairs.
next: After refreshing ProPickle in Chrome, inspect the saved payload's probe_debug for Thursday 16 if the share page still looks wrong.

task: Harden ProPickle time selection settling before court probes
lessons: Court probing should reuse a time-selection settle helper instead of a tiny fixed sleep, because live React widgets may rerender detail state asynchronously.
next: Reload the extension, refresh ProPickle, and use probe_debug if Thursday 16 still does not split by court correctly.

task: Add ProPickle probe_debug summary helper
lessons: A tiny local diagnostic script is enough to turn raw probe_debug rows into accepted/rejected court lists without adding more extension UI.
next: Use the helper on a saved ProPickle payload after a real Chrome refresh if the Thursday 16 result still looks wrong.

task: Add popup action to copy ProPickle probe summary
lessons: For hard-to-verify live DOM scrapers, keep diagnostics hidden in More actions and reveal them only when a saved payload actually contains probe rows.
next: Reload extension, refresh ProPickle, then use More actions -> Copy probe summary if Thursday 16 still needs inspection.

task: Make deep court scans use a visible reader window and fail clearly if hidden
lessons: Chrome throttles hidden booking tabs enough that deep DOM probing should run in a visible reader window or fail loudly instead of silently crawling.
next: Reload the unpacked extension and run a ProPickle deep scan with the reader window visible.

task: Fix ProPickle continuity by isolating each time before court probing
validation: live Chrome read-only ProPickle check: Thu Jul 16 isolated 9-10PM shows COURT 4; isolated 10-11PM shows COURT 1-6; combined 9-11PM shows COURT 4
lessons: Playbypoint time buttons can remain multi-selected; court detail options then represent the intersection for the combined block, so continuity probing must isolate one time slot before reading courts.
next: Reload the unpacked extension and refresh ProPickle deep scan to sync the corrected payload.

task: Polish deep-scan refresh messaging and smoke test venue share routes
validation: Vercel latest production deployment listed ready
production share pages with dev-share returned 404 as expected for invalid token
local production smoke returned 200 for /health and all six /s/dev-share venue routes
errors: Production all-venue share-page smoke needs the real SHARE_TOKEN; pulling full Vercel env was rejected as too broad for this task.
lessons: Deep-scan UI should say reader window, not focused tab, because the provider now opens a dedicated visible window to avoid Chrome throttling.
next: Reload the unpacked extension. For production share-page smoke, use a real copied share link or provide the share token explicitly.
Run web/supabase.sql in Supabase before deploying planner persistence; then test /planner/new on Vercel.
lessons: Planner recommendations should consume cached venue availability only; participant matching can stay server-side and reuse same-court versus any-court confidence.
task: Add When2Meet-style pickleball planner MVP
validation: local smoke: seeded ProPickle cache, created planner event, saved participant, planner page returned 200 with 1 recommendation

next: Reload the unpacked extension to see the new Create Group Planner action, then commit/push if the UX looks right.
errors: Temporary dev server on another port could not start because a Next dev server was already running on localhost:3007; smoked against the existing server instead.
lessons: Availability links and planner links need separate labels and entry points so secret availability tokens are not confused with group planner URLs.
task: Clarify availability links versus group planner links
validation: local route smoke on existing localhost:3007 for /, /planner/new, and planner venue query variants

task: Split planner group overlap from venue court matches
errors: Initial group-time minimum-duration fixture expected a remaining 60-minute solo band but only created 30 minutes; corrected fixture and reran tests.
lessons: Planner UI should always show participant overlap separately from venue/cache matches so empty venue cache does not make the event look broken.
next: Preview the planner event page with saved participants, then push branch for Vercel preview before merging to main.

task: Add planner edit passwords and continuous heatmap
errors: Direct plannerStore fixture tests required explicit TS relative imports for Node's built-in ESM test runner; Next build still passed after the import change.
lessons: Planner participant recovery should be name-and-password based while keeping localStorage edit tokens for same-browser convenience; public views must never expose edit tokens or password hashes.
next: Run web/supabase.sql in Supabase before testing password saves on Vercel preview, then verify incognito participant recovery.

task: Keep planner event pages loading before Supabase password migration
lessons: Planner event reads should tolerate the old Supabase participant schema so creating a planner does not crash before the edit-password migration is applied; participant saves can still report the migration requirement clearly.
next: Push preview and retest planner creation; run web/supabase.sql before testing participant password saves.

task: Make planner edit passwords optional
lessons: A planner participant should save without a password and rely on the local edit token; an optional password is only for cross-device recovery, and a passwordless identity cannot safely be reclaimed from another device.
next: Push the branch preview, apply web/supabase.sql, and verify passwordless save plus optional incognito recovery before merging to main.

task: Make planner recovery errors private and recommendations venue-based
errors: Typecheck found that merged venue windows intentionally omit court confidence while intersectIntervals still required the full interval type; narrowed the helper parameter and reran all checks.
lessons: Planner recommendations should consolidate court rows into venue-level windows; court continuity is supporting confidence, not the recommendation identity or ranking unit.
Participant access failures should use one generic message rather than reveal whether a display name exists or has recovery configured.
next: Push the preview branch and manually verify generic recovery errors plus one Venue Matches result per venue/time.
